response: Japan, a major advanced economy with a population of 125 million, has a total GDP of $4.2 trillion, recording 1.1% GDP growth alongside a remarkably low unemployment rate of 2.6% that signals a robust domestic labor market. The country currently faces 3.2% inflation and a very high debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%, though it continues to post strong long-term metrics including an average life expectancy of 84.6 years and annual exports totaling $756 billion.
